other_observers: About 50 birders observed this bird over three days that it was present, however I was the only one there when it was initially found
description: I observed a bird consistent in size and shape with a warbler.  It was black on the head, back and tail, with a bright red belly, bold white wing bars and white outer tail feathers.  The bird showed a half ark of white under the eye.  Vent area showed dark feathers over a pale gray base.  Bird flew around between a variety of trees but allowed for good observations in sunlight before moving to an area where it was backlit.  The bird did not call while I observed it.  It appeared to be foraging on bugs in the trees and on leaves.  It was in the company of a mixed flock of Audubon's and Myrtle Yellow-rumped Warblers (plus a female Pine Warbler).
photographer: Photographer: Cathy Sheeter, Cathy Sheeter, Cathy Sheeter, Cathy Sheeter, Cathy Sheeter
